description: A cross-platform coffee-shop ordering app — browse the menu, filter by category, build a cart, and place orders. Demonstrates a responsive bottom-TabBar navigation shell, page view-models wired through Uno.Extensions Navigation with shared cart/orders state, and a warm light/dark theme — from one Uno single project targeting iOS, Android, WebAssembly, and desktop.

messages.content:
Generate a production-ready modern Coffee Shop mobile app (home + ordering flow) with:
- mock state
- a warm, cozy UI (beige/coffee tones) that also has a proper dark theme
- a bottom navigation bar (Home, Menu, Cart, Orders)

Home screen:
- hero image carousel with a primary "Order Now" CTA
- Today's Specials (horizontal scrolling cards)
- category chips
- featured products (image, name, description, price, +Add button)
- a cart summary strip and a rich empty-cart state

Navigation:
- a Menu screen with a working category filter
- a Cart screen (line items, quantity controls, totals, Place Order)
- an Orders screen with order history and status

Include:
- full working code
- navigation between screens that shares one cart/orders state
- seeded sample items (Latte, Cappuccino, Croissant, Iced Matcha)
Here's your **BrewHouse** app! It's built and running in the preview.

suggestedPrompts: Add a product detail page with navigation
Add a search box to the menu
Show a running item count badge on the Cart tab

messages.content: Generate a production-ready modern Coffee Shop mobile app (home + ordering flow) with a warm cozy UI plus a dark theme, a bottom nav bar (Home, Menu, Cart, Orders), a category filter, a cart with quantity controls and Place Order, and an order-history screen. Share one cart/orders state across screens and seed sample items.

I've created a cross-platform Uno Platform app at BrewHouse/BrewHouse.csproj (a single project targeting iOS, Android, WebAssembly, and desktop). It uses Uno.Extensions Navigation and Uno Toolkit on the Uno Simple theme, with a warm light/dark palette.

**Shell** — Presentation/Shell.xaml is the navigation root; Presentation/MainPage.xaml is a bottom utu:TabBar shell (Home / Menu / Cart / Orders) over a region-based content area (uen:Region.Attached) that Navigation injects the active page into.

**Pages & view-models** — HomePage, MenuPage, CartPage and OrdersPage each bind a page view-model (HomePageData / MenuPageData / CartPageData / OrdersPageData) registered via ViewMap in App.xaml.cs and constructed by DI with an INavigator for the cross-tab navigation commands (Order Now, View Cart, Browse Menu). Buttons inside item templates reach their page VM through the Toolkit {utu:ItemsControlBinding} markup extension.

**State & data** — Presentation/MockData/ holds the seeded catalogue and a shared AppState (registered as a singleton) that owns the cart and order history, so adding to the cart on one screen updates the cart strip, Cart page and Orders page together. Models.cs holds the entity classes; RelayCommand.cs the ICommand helper.

**Theming** — ThemeColors.xaml defines the cream/espresso (light) and dark-roast (dark) palettes as ThemeDictionaries, merged onto the Simple theme via SimpleToolkitTheme ColorOverrideSource and referenced everywhere with {ThemeResource}; Presentation/Converters.cs maps an order status to a semantic indicator-dot colour. The app icon and splash are generated from the SVGs in Assets/ by Uno.Resizetizer.

The app is built and running. The user may ask me to modify it.
